text: A community health nurse is planning activities to reduce the incidence of hypertension in a community. Which intervention is most appropriate?
option_b: Conduct blood pressure screening and health education
rationale: Screening and health education promote early detection and prevention of hypertension.

text: A nurse visits a family with a newborn baby. Which action best promotes disease prevention?
option_b: Educate the parents about the childhood immunization schedule
option_c: Advise the family to avoid clinic visits
rationale: Educating parents about routine immunization helps prevent vaccine-preventable diseases.

text: A community experiences an increase in malaria cases during the rainy season. Which health education message is most appropriate?
rationale: Using insecticide-treated mosquito nets is an effective method of preventing malaria transmission.

text: A nurse identifies several children in a village with signs of malnutrition. What should be the nurse's priority action?
option_b: Provide nutrition education and growth monitoring
rationale: Nutrition education and regular growth monitoring help prevent and manage childhood malnutrition.

text: During a home visit the nurse notices uncovered refuse around a house. Which health problem is most likely to result?
rationale: Poor refuse disposal promotes breeding of disease vectors and increases infection risk.

text: A nurse is conducting health education on proper handwashing. Which disease is most effectively prevented through this practice?
rationale: Proper hand hygiene reduces the transmission of organisms that cause diarrheal diseases.

text: A pregnant woman attends her first antenatal clinic. Which intervention best promotes maternal and fetal health?
option_d: Advise complete bed rest throughout pregnancy
rationale: Regular antenatal care allows early detection and management of pregnancy-related complications.

text: A community health nurse discovers several children have incomplete immunization records. What is the best nursing action?
option_c: Advise parents to wait until adulthood
rationale: Catch-up immunization protects children who missed scheduled vaccines.

text: A family obtains drinking water from an unprotected stream. Which advice should the nurse give?
rationale: Boiling water destroys many disease-causing microorganisms and makes it safer to drink.

text: A nurse is investigating an outbreak of food poisoning in a community. Which action should be performed first?
option_b: Identify the source of the outbreak
rationale: Identifying the source helps control the outbreak and prevent additional cases.

text: A school nurse teaches students about oral hygiene. What is the primary purpose of this education?
rationale: Good oral hygiene helps prevent dental caries and gum disease.

text: A community health nurse visits an elderly client living alone. Which assessment should receive priority?
option_b: Risk of falls and home safety
rationale: Older adults are at increased risk of falls

text: A nurse is planning a health promotion campaign on smoking cessation. What is the primary goal?
option_b: Reduce the incidence of smoking-related diseases
rationale: Smoking cessation reduces the risk of cardiovascular disease

text: A community reports an increase in cholera cases after flooding. Which intervention is most effective?
option_a: Promote safe water and proper sanitation
rationale: Safe water and sanitation interrupt the transmission of cholera.

text: A mother asks why exclusive breastfeeding is recommended for the first six months of life. What is the best response?
option_b: It provides optimal nutrition and immune protection
option_c: It eliminates the need for immunization
rationale: Exclusive breastfeeding provides complete nutrition and antibodies that protect infants from infections.

text: A nurse observes stagnant water around several homes. Which disease should the nurse be most concerned about?
rationale: Stagnant water provides breeding sites for mosquitoes that transmit malaria.

text: A community health nurse is evaluating a vaccination campaign. Which outcome indicates success?
rationale: High immunization coverage reduces the spread of vaccine-preventable diseases.

text: A client with pulmonary tuberculosis is receiving treatment at home. Which instruction is most appropriate?
option_b: Complete the full course of treatment
rationale: Completing the full course of tuberculosis treatment prevents relapse and drug resistance.

text: A nurse conducts a community screening program for diabetes. What is the primary purpose of screening?
option_b: Detect disease early for timely intervention
rationale: Screening identifies individuals with disease or those at risk before complications develop.

text: A community health nurse encourages residents to participate in regular physical activity. Which health outcome is expected?
rationale: Regular physical activity lowers the risk of obesity
